info.title: Secure Templated Actions Runtime (STAR)
info.description:
Secure Templated Actions Runtime (STAR) is a hardened execution microservice designed to expose a strictly allow-listed action surface for automation platforms and internal systems.

It enforces:
- Explicit action registry (no arbitrary command execution)
- Defense-in-depth middleware stack
- Deterministic response envelopes
- Centralized error taxonomy
- Observability-first architecture
- Runtime-aware OpenAPI contract generation

get.description:
Health check endpoint.

Returns a minimal readiness payload as defined in the SRS.

Returns:
    A JSONResponse containing the readiness status, e.g. `{"status": "ok"}`.

Prometheus metrics exposition endpoint.

This endpoint returns the current Prometheus metrics snapshot for the
running process using the `prometheus_client` library.

Returns:
    A Starlette Response with the metrics payload and Prometheus content
    type (`CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST`).

schema.example:
# HELP http_requests_total ...
# TYPE http_requests_total counter
http_requests_total 42
# HELP ...

get.summary: List available actions grouped by module with optional filtering.
get.description:
Discover registered actions grouped by module.

Query parameters:
- `q`: Optional free-text search over action name, summary, description, and effective tags.
- `tags`: Optional CSV tag filter, for example `hashing,checksum`. Tokens are trimmed, normalized to lowercase, and deduplicated.
- `match`: Optional tag matching mode: `any` or `all`.

Behavior:
- If `tags` is provided and `match` is omitted, matching defaults to `any`.
- Providing `match` without `tags` returns `INVALID_PARAMS`.
- When `q` and `tags` are both present, filters are combined with logical AND.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Optional free-text filter over action name, summary, description, and effective tags.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Optional CSV tags filter. Example: `hashing,checksum`. Tokens are trimmed, lowercased, and deduplicated.

parameters.name: match
parameters.description: Optional tag match mode. Allowed: `any` or `all`. Requires `tags`; defaults to `any` when omitted.

modules.description: Cryptographic primitives for hashing, integrity checks, and file encryption workflows

actions.description: Compute the SHA-256 digest of one managed file using sha256sum
Compute SHA-256 digests for multiple managed files in a single action

get.summary: Get public specification of an action
get.description:
Retrieve the full public contract of a DSL-defined action.

Includes:
- Arguments
- Flags
- Outputs
- Request/response schemas

post.summary: Execute a registered action with given parameters.
post.description:
Executes a registered STAR action within the secure sandbox environment.

Set the target action using the `action_id` path parameter.

### Supported Actions

- `base.analyze.count_file_chars`: Count file characters
- `base.analyze.count_file_lines`: Count file lines
- `base.analyze.count_file_words`: Count file words
- `base.analyze.inspect_file_type`: Inspect file type
- `base.analyze.preview_file_end`: Preview file end lines
- `base.analyze.preview_file_start`: Preview file start lines
- `base.analyze.search_file_lines`: Search lines in a file
- `base.crypto.decrypt_file_aes256`: Decrypt a file encrypted with AES-256
- `base.crypto.encrypt_file_aes256`: Encrypt a file with AES-256
- `base.crypto.hash_file`: Hash a file with a selected algorithm
- `base.crypto.sha256_file`: Hash one file with SHA-256
- `base.crypto.sha256_files`: Hash multiple files with SHA-256
- `base.random.gen_bytes_file`: Generate random bytes as file
- `base.random.gen_token_base64`: Generate a random Base64 token
- `base.random.gen_token_hex`: Generate a random hex token
- `base.random.gen_uuid`: Generate a kernel-backed UUIDv4

base.analyze.count_file_chars.description:
Count characters in a managed file

#### Args

- `input_file` (`file_id`): Managed file ID to measure; **\*required**

#### Flags

- _No flags_

#### Request Options

- `stdout_as_file` (`bool`): Store sanitized stdout as `outputs.stdout_file` when enabled and allowed by the action; default: `false`;

#### Args

#### Flags

- _No flags_

base.analyze.search_file_lines.summary: base.analyze.search_file_lines: Search lines in a file
base.analyze.search_file_lines.description:
Search matching lines in a managed file using grep semantics

#### Args

- `input_file` (`file_id`): Managed file ID to inspect; **\*required**
- `pattern` (`string`): Search pattern passed directly to grep; **\*required**

#### Flags

- `regex`: Interpret pattern as an extended grep regular expression; default: `false`
- `ignore_case`: Perform case-insensitive matching; default: `false`
- `add_line_numbers`: Prefix each match with its line number; default: `false`
- `invert_match`: Return non-matching lines instead of matches; default: `false`

#### Request Options

- `stdout_as_file` (`bool`): Store sanitized stdout as `outputs.stdout_file` when enabled and allowed by the action; default: `false`;

base.crypto.decrypt_file_aes256.summary: base.crypto.decrypt_file_aes256: Decrypt a file encrypted with AES-256
base.crypto.decrypt_file_aes256.description:
Decrypt an AES-256-CBC managed file using the same passphrase used during encryption

#### Args

- `input_file` (`file_id`): Managed encrypted file ID to decrypt; **\*required**
- `password` (`string`): Password used to derive the decryption key; **\*required**

#### Flags

- _No flags_

#### Request Options

- `stdout_as_file` (`bool`): Store sanitized stdout as `outputs.stdout_file` when enabled and allowed by the action; default: `false`;

base.crypto.encrypt_file_aes256.summary: base.crypto.encrypt_file_aes256: Encrypt a file with AES-256
base.crypto.encrypt_file_aes256.description:
Encrypt a managed file using AES-256-CBC with PBKDF2-derived key material

#### Args

- `input_file` (`file_id`): Managed file ID to encrypt; **\*required**
- `password` (`string`): Password used to derive the encryption key; **\*required**

#### Flags

- _No flags_

#### Request Options

- `stdout_as_file` (`bool`): Store sanitized stdout as `outputs.stdout_file` when enabled and allowed by the action; default: `false`;

base.crypto.hash_file.summary: base.crypto.hash_file: Hash a file with a selected algorithm
base.crypto.hash_file.description:
Compute a cryptographic digest for a managed file using a selectable OpenSSL hash algorithm

#### Args

- `input_file` (`file_id`): Managed file ID to hash; **\*required**
- `algorithm` (`string`): Digest algorithm to apply; default: `sha256`

#### Flags

- _No flags_

#### Request Options

- `stdout_as_file` (`bool`): Store sanitized stdout as `outputs.stdout_file` when enabled and allowed by the action; default: `false`;

base.crypto.sha256_file.description:

base.crypto.sha256_files.summary: base.crypto.sha256_files: Hash multiple files with SHA-256

requestBody.description:
Request body containing action-specific `params` and request-level execution options.

The target action is selected via the `action_id` path parameter. Use `stdout_as_file=true` to store sanitized stdout as `outputs.stdout_file` when the selected action allows it. Select an example below to inspect parameter contracts per action.

error.message: HTTP method not allowed for this path.

post.description:
**Upload and securely persist a file using STAR-managed storage.**

This endpoint accepts multipart/form-data uploads and enforces a strict validation pipeline before persisting any data.

Processing steps:
- Stream upload to temporary storage
- Compute SHA256 checksum
- Detect MIME type using server-side inspection
- Validate extension ↔ MIME mapping
- Reject executable or unsafe file types
- Enforce maximum file size limits
- Persist blob and metadata atomically

Checksum verification:
- An optional `checksum` field may be provided
- The value MUST be the SHA256 hash (hex-encoded) of the file contents
- If provided, STAR verifies integrity before accepting the upload
- Mismatches result in request rejection

Storage model:
- Files are stored as immutable blobs
- Metadata is persisted as a JSON sidecar document
- Each file is identified by a UUID

Security guarantees:
- No trust in client-provided MIME types
- Strict extension-to-MIME validation
- Executable content is rejected by default
- Atomic write operations prevent partial persistence

get.description:
**List STAR-managed files with cursor pagination and deterministic ordering.**

Supports filtering by `status`, `mime_type`, and `extension`, with sorting by `created_at` and stable tiebreaking by file id.

**Retrieve metadata for a previously uploaded file managed by STAR.**

This endpoint provides read-only access to file metadata stored in the STAR filesystem-backed storage model. It does not return the file contents.

Behavior:
- Validates the provided file identifier (UUID)
- Loads metadata from STAR-managed storage
- Returns structured metadata in a standard response envelope

Storage model:
- Files are stored as immutable blobs
- Metadata is persisted as a JSON sidecar document
- Each file is uniquely identified by a UUID

Security considerations:
- No direct filesystem paths are exposed
- No file content is returned by this endpoint
- Access is controlled via STAR authentication middleware

Use cases:
- Verify upload success
- Inspect file properties (size, type, checksum)
- Integrate with automation workflows (e.g., n8n)

delete.description:
**Hard-delete a previously uploaded file managed by STAR.**

This endpoint resolves the file through STAR metadata and removes both storage artifacts in strict order:
- blob (`files/blobs/file_<uuid>.bin`)
- metadata (`files/meta/file_<uuid>.json`)

Deletion is only allowed when file metadata is in `ready` state.

get.description:
**Stream the binary contents of a previously uploaded file managed by STAR.**

This endpoint resolves the file through STAR metadata, validates that the file is in ready state, and returns the blob as a streamed download response.

Behavior:
- Validates the provided file identifier (UUID)
- Loads metadata from STAR-managed storage
- Verifies that the file is available for download
- Streams the file content without loading the entire blob into memory

Security considerations:
- No direct filesystem paths are exposed
- Only STAR-managed file identifiers are accepted
- Access is controlled via STAR authentication middleware

ActionSummarySchema.title: ActionSummarySchema
ActionSummarySchema.description:
Public summary payload for one registered action.

Attributes:
    action: Short DSL action name.
    action_id: Fully qualified runtime action name.
    summary: Optional short summary.
    description: Optional long description.
    tags: Effective public action tags.

DeleteFileData.title: DeleteFileData
DeleteFileData.description:
Success payload for `DELETE /v1/files/{id}`.

Attributes:
    file: Structured delete outcome.

DeleteFileResult.title: DeleteFileResult
DeleteFileResult.description:
Delete result payload for a previously stored file.

Attributes:
    id: UUID of the deleted file.
    deleted: Deletion success flag.

ErrorInfo.title: ErrorInfo
ErrorInfo.description:
Standard error payload returned on failed requests.

Attributes:
    code: Machine-readable error code.
    message: Human-readable error message.
    details: Optional structured details payload.

ExecuteActionData.title: ExecuteActionData
ExecuteActionData.description:
Typed success payload for the `POST /v1/actions/{action_id}` endpoint.

Attributes:
    exit_code: Process return code.
    stdout: Sanitized stdout text or base64 payload.
    stdout_encoding: Encoding label for stdout.
    stderr: Sanitized stderr text or base64 payload.
    stderr_encoding: Encoding label for stderr.
    exec_time: Total execution time in seconds.
    pid: Process identifier when available.
    truncated: Whether stdout or stderr was truncated.
    redacted: Whether output was redacted.
    outputs: Materialized file outputs, if any.

stdout_as_file.title: Stdout As File
stdout_as_file.description: When true, STAR stores sanitized stdout as a managed text file under outputs.stdout_file if the action allows it.

ExecuteActionRequest.title: ExecuteActionRequest
ExecuteActionRequest.description:
Client request body for executing a STAR action.

Attributes:
    params: Action parameters.
    stdout_as_file: Whether sanitized stdout should be stored as a managed
        file when the action allows it.

FileListData.title: FileListData
FileListData.description:
Success payload for `GET /v1/files`.

Attributes:
    files: File metadata records included in the current page.
    pagination: Cursor pagination metadata.

FileMetadata.title: FileMetadata
FileMetadata.description:
Typed metadata persisted for each stored file.

Attributes:
    id: Stable UUID assigned by STAR for the uploaded file.
    original_filename: Client-supplied filename after basename normalization.
    stored_filename: Internal blob filename persisted by STAR.
    mime_type: Server-detected MIME type in `type/subtype` form.
    extension: Normalized lowercase extension including leading dot.
    size_bytes: Persisted file size in bytes.
    sha256: Lowercase SHA-256 digest as 64 hex characters.
    created_at: UTC timestamp when the record was created.
    updated_at: UTC timestamp when the record was last updated.
    status: Lifecycle state of the file metadata.

GetActionData.title: GetActionData
GetActionData.description:
Success payload for `GET /v1/actions/{action_id}`.

Attributes:
    action: Short DSL action name.
    action_id: Fully qualified runtime action name.
    summary: Optional short summary.
    description: Optional long description.
    tags: Effective public action tags.
    allow_stdout_as_file: Whether sanitized stdout may be stored as a
        managed file.
    args: Serialized argument definitions.
    flags: Serialized flag definitions.
    outputs: Serialized output definitions.
    params_contract: Public params contract.
    params_example: Public params example payload.
    response_contract: Public response contract.
    response_example: Public response example payload.

ListActionsData.title: ListActionsData
ListActionsData.description:
Success payload for `GET /v1/actions`.

Attributes:
    modules: Discovery summaries grouped by module.

ModuleSummarySchema.title: ModuleSummarySchema
ModuleSummarySchema.description:
Public summary payload for one DSL module.

Attributes:
    module: Bare module name.
    module_id: Fully qualified module identifier.
    namespace: Dot-separated namespace string.
    namespace_path: Namespace segments.
    description: Public module description.
    tags: Public module tags.
    authors: Public module authors.
    actions: Public action summaries within the module.

Pagination.title: Pagination
Pagination.description:
Cursor pagination metadata for file listing responses.

Attributes:
    count: Number of files returned in the current page.
    next_cursor: Opaque cursor for the next page, if available.

UploadFileData.title: UploadFileData
UploadFileData.description:
Success payload for `POST /v1/files`.

Attributes:
    file: Persisted metadata for the uploaded file.

ResponseEnvelope.description:
Standard HTTP response envelope used across services.

Fields follow the README contract: `success`, `data`, and `error`.
Request correlation is performed via the `X-Request-Id` header injected
by middleware; the JSON body deliberately omits `request_id`.

Attributes:
    success: Whether the request completed successfully.
    data: Success payload when available.
    error: Structured error payload when request fails.
